page: Microsoft Azure E2ds_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz. This instance configuration provides 2 vCPUs derived from 1 physical core with hyper-threading enabled. It features 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per vCPU. The hardware configuration includes 77 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, while operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

Benchmark data highlights a distinct performance profile characterized by moderate single-core capabilities but constrained multi-core and memory throughput. Single-core metrics, such as stress-ng single-core CPU performance, Geekbench single-core, and single-threaded gzip compression, score in the average range. However, due to the low physical core count, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and Passmark CPU scores, fall into the bottom tier. Memory bandwidth tests also demonstrate lower-tier performance, with uncached read and write speeds categorized as poor.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the E2ds_v5 offers a high memory density relative to its low compute footprint, making it a viable option for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel processing power. The inclusion of 77 GB of local storage provides a built-in caching tier without requiring external disk attachments. This server is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers with moderate throughput requirements, lightweight enterprise applications, and memory-intensive utility workloads.
description: The Microsoft Azure E2ds_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor with 2 vCPUs and 16.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, it includes 77 GB of local storage and supports Premium Storage, but lacks GPU resources. Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core CPU execution and single-threaded compression, contrasted by poor multi-core and memory bandwidth results due to its single physical core design. This resource profile offers qualitative cost efficiency for memory-heavy, low-compute tasks. It is best suited for small-scale databases, caching layers, and lightweight memory-intensive applications.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring high RAM-to-vCPU density and local storage for lightweight, memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
